systemic_losses: P1: String of Nickels (Yellow Pot) - Appears to be a systemic loss, with only dried remnants visible by T-3.
P2: Mexican Mint (Black Pot) - Systemic loss, no plant material visible.
P3: Pothos (Black Pot | White Rabbit anchor) - Systemic loss, no plant material or anchor visible.
P4: Silver Guest (Black Pot | Shared with P2) - Systemic loss, no plant material visible.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Not present as a living specimen; only dried, stringy remnants in dry, cracked soil by T-3. Declared Systemic Loss.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Not present; only bare soil and eggshells. Declared Systemic Loss.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Not present; no plant material or white rabbit anchor visible. Declared Systemic Loss.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Not present; only bare soil and eggshells. Declared Systemic Loss.

P1_Yellow_Pot.earliest: Contains dark soil, no discernible plant material due to extreme darkness.
P1_Yellow_Pot.t-4: Contains dark soil, very faint, dark, possibly dried plant matter or debris on surface.
P1_Yellow_Pot.t-3: Contains dry, cracked soil with clear, faint, dark, stringy remnants of what was likely the String of Nickels. No living tissue.

P2_P3_P4_Black_Pot.earliest: Contains dark soil with some lighter, possibly reflective, debris (eggshells) on the surface. No discernible plant material.
P2_P3_P4_Black_Pot.t-4: Contains dark soil with lighter debris (eggshells) still present, possibly slightly more defined. No discernible plant material.
P2_P3_P4_Black_Pot.t-3: Contains dark soil with clearly defined scattered eggshells. No living plant material visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture_P1: Transitions from dark/possibly damp (EARLIEST) to visibly dry and cracked (T-3).
biome_observations.soil_texture_P2_P3_P4: Appears dark in initial images, no clear dampness or cracking observed. Covered by eggshells.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: None visible in any image due to darkness and subsequent obscuration.
biome_observations.debris_on_desk: Not clearly visible in initial images. Entire scene obscured from T-2 onwards.

temporal_deltas.changes: Slight increase in overall image brightness. Soil in the yellow pot (P1) appears to have faint dark debris/matter and looks slightly drier. No significant change in the black pot (P2/P3/P4) contents, though eggshells remain.

Significant increase in image brightness, allowing for more detail. Soil in the yellow pot (P1) is now visibly dry and cracked, with clearer remnants of what was likely the String of Nickels. Eggshells in the black pot (P2/P3/P4) are more distinct. No living plants are visible in either pot.
Complete loss of visual information; the image is entirely black. This indicates a camera malfunction, complete blackout, or the scene being entirely removed/covered.
No change; the image remains entirely black, indicating continued loss of visual information.

The image transitions from black to a uniform grey. Still no discernible objects, plants, or pots. This suggests the entire setup is no longer in view, has been replaced by a blank grey surface, or the camera is pointed elsewhere/covered.

visual_health_inference.P1_String_of_Nickels: Inferred to be dead due to desiccation and decay, evidenced by dry, cracked soil and only stringy remnants visible by T-3.
visual_health_inference.P2_Mexican_Mint: Inferred to be a systemic loss; no plant material visible, only bare soil and eggshells.
visual_health_inference.P3_Pothos: Inferred to be a systemic loss; no plant material or scale anchor visible.
visual_health_inference.P4_Silver_Guest: Inferred to be a systemic loss; no plant material visible, only bare soil and eggshells.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome_health: The biome shows severe degradation and systemic loss of all registered plants by T-3. The subsequent complete obscuration of the scene from T-2 onwards prevents any further assessment but strongly implies the entire setup is no longer present or observable.

anomalies: Eggshells present in the black pot (P2/P3/P4) are a 'New Introduction/Intervention' not listed in the registry.
Complete loss of visual data in images T-2 and T-1 (entirely black).
Uniform grey image in CURRENT, indicating the entire setup is no longer in view or has been replaced by a blank surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: The chronological audit reveals a rapid and severe decline in the observed botanical biome. In the initial images (EARLIEST, T-4, T-3), the String of Nickels (P1) in the yellow pot visibly deteriorates, showing signs of severe desiccation with dry, cracked soil and only faint, stringy remnants by T-3. The other registered plants – Mexican Mint, Pothos, and Silver Guest (P2, P3, P4) – are entirely absent from the black pot, which contains only bare soil and scattered eggshells. The white rabbit scale anchor is also missing. From T-2 onwards, the camera feed becomes completely obscured, first presenting as entirely black images (T-2, T-1) and finally as a uniform grey image (CURRENT). This sequence of events strongly suggests a complete systemic loss of all expected plant specimens and the subsequent removal or complete obscuring of the entire botanical observation setup. No living plant material is visible at any point in the sequence, and the environment appears to have been abandoned or dismantled.
vision_report.confidence: High. While initial images are dark, the progression of desiccation in P1 and the consistent absence of other plants, coupled with the complete obscuration of the scene, provides strong visual evidence for the inferences made.
